content:
# Real-world benchmark: combined LUT GPU grading pipeline — clip 0057 (2026-04-04)

**Date:** 2026-04-04
**Pipeline version:** after combined 97³ CUDA 3D texture LUT (PR #33)

## Test clip

- File: `DJI_20251101112849_0057_D.MP4`
- Resolution: 3840×2160 (4K), HEVC
- Frame rate: 119.88 fps
- Duration: 41.3s → 4,943 frames (3× longer than clip 0055)

## Results

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total wall time | **13 min 42.888s** |
| User time | 32 min 49.812s |
| Sys time | 3 min 54.355s |
| Output path | `working/graded/DJI_20251101112849_0057_D_graded_combined-lut-perf_20260404.mp4` |

## Pipeline stages

| Stage | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| Auto-calibration keyframes | 164 (30-frame interval) |
| Depth keyframes detected (pass 1) | 560 |
| Grading pass duration | ~7m 20s for 4,943 frames (~11.2 fps) |

## Comparison vs clip 0055

| Metric | Clip 0055 (1,671 frames) | Clip 0057 (4,943 frames) |
|--------|--------------------------|--------------------------|
| Wall time | 2 min 6.9s | 13 min 42.9s |
| Overall fps | ~13.2 | ~6.0 |
| Depth keyframe % | ~1.2% | 11.3% (560 keyframes) |

**Key finding:** Clip 0057 has a much higher depth-keyframe rate (11.3% vs ~1.2%) due to more scene changes. This explains the lower overall fps — more depth inference invocations per frame. Depth inference remains the bottleneck; the combined LUT grading kernel itself is not the issue.
